growth_records.strain: not specified (experimental values from Blank et al. 2005 chemostat)

up.method: experimental glucose consumption rate cited from Blank et al. 2005 (used as model glucose uptake constraint)

mfa.note: experimental constraint from Blank et al. 2005; applied in all iZN522 simulations

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Growth rate (biomass), anaerobic CDM - model prediction
mfa.note: FBA prediction vs experimental 0.4/h; NOT experimental

growth_records.cite: Nikuiyan, Zahra et al. Reconstruction of a genome-scale metabolic model for Streptococcus zooepidemicus: Comparison with Corynebacterium glutamicum to study hyaluronic acid production. PLOS One (2025). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0335509. [PMC12755811]
growth_records.snippet: The predicted growth rate by the model in anaerobic conditions and CDM culture medium is close to the reported growth rate by Blank et al. ... The glucose uptake rate was set to 18.56 mmol.gDCW-1.h-1 ... based on previous experimental data
growth_records.notes: CITED experimental data, not measured in this paper. Experimental mu=0.4 h-1 (anaerobic, CDM) from Blank et al., Biotechnol Bioeng 2005 'Stable production of hyaluronic acid in Streptococcus zooepidemicus chemostats operated at high dilution rate' (ref 51). doubling = ln2/0.4 = 1.73 h. Experimental glucose uptake 18.56 mmol/gDCW/h also from Blank et al. (ref 51). Model iZN522 predicted mu=0.3732 h-1 (prediction, excluded from experimental fields). A comparison C. glutamicum glucose uptake of 4.67 mmol/gDCW/h in CGXII was used as a model constraint (source not specified as in-paper measurement; not recorded).

up.compound: initial substrate S0 (fitted total sugars available)

sec.method: reparameterized-logistic fit to measured HPLC time-course (DataFit, Levenberg-Marquardt)

growth_records.cite: Vázquez, José A. et al. Hyaluronic acid production by Streptococcus zooepidemicus in marine by-products media from mussel processing wastewaters and tuna peptone viscera. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-46. [PMC2901256]
growth_records.snippet: medium A had the highest maximum biomass production (Xm = 5.17 g l-1) maximum growth rate (vx = 1.32 g l-1 h-1)... Xm 5.17; vx 1.32; Hm 3.07; vh 0.82; Lm 35.12; vl 8.53; S0 45.81; Yx/s 0.11; me 0.01(NS); Yh/x 0.58; Yl/x 6.61
growth_records.notes: Batch fermentation, 37C, pH 6.7, no aeration. Reported params (Table 2) fitted by reparameterized-logistic + Luedeking-Piret/maintenance model to duplicate measured time-courses (biomass by OD700->dry weight, LA by HPLC, HA precipitation assay, sugars by phenol-sulphuric). vx/vh/vl are MAXIMUM VOLUMETRIC rates (g/L/h), NOT specific. mu computed from logistic form X=Xm/(1+exp(2+(4*vx/Xm)*(lambda-t))): mu_max=4*vx/Xm=4*1.32/5.17=1.021/h; td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.021=0.679 h. Specific sugar uptake qS=mu/Yxs+me=1.021/0.11+0.01=9.29 g/g/h => /0.18016=51.6 mmol/gDW/h (flagged implausibly high, consequence of high fitted mu). Yl/s and Yh/s derived as Yl/x*Yxs and Yh/x*Yxs. me for medium A =0.01 g/g/h (not significant).

sec.method: reparameterized-logistic fit to measured HPLC time-course

growth_records.cite: Vázquez, José A. et al. Hyaluronic acid production by Streptococcus zooepidemicus in marine by-products media from mussel processing wastewaters and tuna peptone viscera. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-46. [PMC2901256]
growth_records.notes: Same batch setup as medium A; tryptone replaced by tuna-viscera peptone. mu_max=4*vx/Xm=4*0.91/3.81=0.955/h; td=0.6931/0.955=0.726 h. qS=mu/Yxs+me=0.955/0.09+0.06=10.68 g/g/h => /0.18016=59.3 mmol/gDW/h (derived, high). me=0.06 g/g/h (NS). Yields Yl/s, Yh/s = product yield_x * Yxs. All params fitted from duplicate measured time-courses.

growth_records.cite: Vázquez, José A. et al. Hyaluronic acid production by Streptococcus zooepidemicus in marine by-products media from mussel processing wastewaters and tuna peptone viscera. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-46. [PMC2901256]
growth_records.notes: Glucose replaced by MPW glycogen; strain secretes extracellular amylase to liberate glucose. mu_max=4*vx/Xm=4*0.93/3.55=1.048/h; td=0.6931/1.048=0.661 h. qS=mu/Yxs+me=1.048/0.11+0.58=10.11 g/g/h => /0.18016=56.1 mmol/gDW/h (derived, high). me=0.58 g/g/h (significant, higher maintenance on glycogen substrate). Params fitted from duplicate measured time-courses.

growth_records.snippet: more than 30 and 2.4 g l-1 of LA and HA respectively were obtained in medium D... MEDIUM D: Xm 3.67+/-0.06; vx 0.81+/-0.06; lambda_x 3.46; Hm 2.46; vh 0.53; Lm 30.83; vl 7.73; S0 51.47; Yx/s 0.10; me 0.43+/-0.13; Yh/x 0.65; Yl/x 8.43
growth_records.notes: Fully marine low-cost medium (MPW glycogen + tuna peptone); best HA yield-per-biomass (0.65). mu_max=4*vx/Xm=4*0.81/3.67=0.883/h; td=0.6931/0.883=0.785 h. qS=mu/Yxs+me=0.883/0.10+0.43=9.26 g/g/h => /0.18016=51.4 mmol/gDW/h (derived, high). me=0.43 g/g/h (significant). Highest measured biomass 3.67, HA 2.46, LA 30.83 g/L reported in abstract/conclusions. Params fitted from duplicate measured time-courses.

growth_records.medium: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ), no aeration

up.units: g sugar / g cell / h (derived qS)
up.method: Somogyi-Nelson total reducing sugars; sugars exhausted (~0.05 g/L) by 16 h

sec.units: main glucose-catabolism metabolite (pH drop); not quantified

growth_records.cite: Oliveira AH et al. Cashew apple juice as microbial cultivation medium for non-immunogenic hyaluronic acid production. Brazilian Journal of Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1590/S1517-83822014005000017. [PMC3958174]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Cashew Apple Juice (CAJ) without aeration 0.12 g/L HA. Table 2: CAJ (*without aeration) YP/S 0.001, YX/S 0.01, PP 0.008 g/L/h, Px 0.03 g/L/h, muXmax 0.01 h-1, muPmax 0.002 h-1.
growth_records.notes: S. zooepidemicus ATCC 39920 in crude CAJ, Erlenmeyer flask, no forced aeration, 37C, pH ~7 (uncontrolled in flask), 150 rpm. Reported (Table 2): muXmax 0.01 /h, YX/S 0.01, YP/S 0.001, HA productivity PP 0.008 g/L/h, biomass productivity Px 0.03 g/L/h, muPmax 0.002 /h. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.01=69.3 h. Derived substrate uptake qS=muXmax/YX/S=0.01/0.01=1.0 g sugar/g cell/h (marked non-experimental as it is derived from reported mu and Yxs). Final HA titer 0.12 g/L (Table 1); abstract lists 0.15 g/L for the unsupplemented no-aeration case. Sugars (~100 g/L, 10% reducing sugars) exhausted to ~0.05 g/L by 16 h. Lactic acid is main metabolite (pH decrease) but not quantified. HA was low-MW oligomer (viscosity 3.87 cP).

growth_records.medium: Cashew apple juice + 60 g/L yeast extract (CAJY), no aeration

up.units: g sugar / g cell / h (derived qS)
up.method: Somogyi-Nelson; sugars exhausted faster (~4 h) than CAJ

growth_records.cite: Oliveira AH et al. Cashew apple juice as microbial cultivation medium for non-immunogenic hyaluronic acid production. Brazilian Journal of Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1590/S1517-83822014005000017. [PMC3958174]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: CAJY without aeration 0.49 g/L HA. Table 2: CAJY (*without aeration) YP/S 0.006, YX/S 0.01, PP 0.019 g/L/h, Px 0.24 g/L/h, muXmax 0.06 h-1, muPmax 0.002 h-1. Similar but faster (4 h) uptake of sugars was observed on CAJY medium.
growth_records.notes: S. zooepidemicus ATCC 39920 in CAJY (CAJ + 60 g/L yeast extract), Erlenmeyer flask, no forced aeration, 37C, 150 rpm. Reported (Table 2): muXmax 0.06 /h, YX/S 0.01, YP/S 0.006, PP 0.019 g/L/h, Px 0.24 g/L/h, muPmax 0.002 /h. td=0.693/0.06=11.55 h. Derived qS=muXmax/YX/S=0.06/0.01=6.0 g sugar/g cell/h (non-experimental; high value reflects very low reported YX/S 0.01). Final HA 0.49 g/L (Table 1). Sugars exhausted by ~4 h. Biomass and HA ~3-fold higher than plain CAJ.

growth_records.medium: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ), 2 vvm aeration

up.units: g sugar / g cell / h (derived qS)

sec.units: more acetate formed under aeration (little/no ethanol or formate); not quantified

growth_records.cite: Oliveira AH et al. Cashew apple juice as microbial cultivation medium for non-immunogenic hyaluronic acid production. Brazilian Journal of Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1590/S1517-83822014005000017. [PMC3958174]
growth_records.snippet: the sodium hyaluronate concentration increased from 0.12 g/L to 1.88 g/L in CAJ [with 2 vvm aeration]. Table 1: CAJ with 2 vvm 1.88 +/- 0.03 g/L. Table 2: CAJ (**2 vvm) YP/S 0.021, YX/S 0.07, PP 0.118 g/L/h, Px 0.57 g/L/h, muXmax 0.09 h-1,
growth_records.notes: S. zooepidemicus ATCC 39920 in crude CAJ, 3 L BioFLO III fermenter, 2.0 L, 2 vvm aeration, 37C, pH 7.0 (NaOH-controlled), 150 rpm. Reported (Table 2): muXmax 0.09 /h, YX/S 0.07, YP/S 0.021, PP 0.118 g/L/h, Px 0.57 g/L/h, muPmax 0.06 /h. td=0.693/0.09=7.7 h. Derived qS=muXmax/YX/S=0.09/0.07=1.286 g sugar/g cell/h (non-experimental). HA final 1.88 +/- 0.03 g/L (Table 1); aeration was the dominant factor (0.12->1.88 g/L). Under O2, pyruvate flux shifts to acetate (more ATP), boosting HA; viscosity 107 cP. Low-MW HA (<500 kDa).

growth_records.medium: Cashew apple juice + 60 g/L yeast extract (CAJY), 2 vvm aeration

up.units: g sugar / g cell / h (derived qS)

growth_records.cite: Oliveira AH et al. Cashew apple juice as microbial cultivation medium for non-immunogenic hyaluronic acid production. Brazilian Journal of Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1590/S1517-83822014005000017. [PMC3958174]
growth_records.snippet: maximum specific growth rate (muXmax) was much lower on CAJ (0.09 h-1) as compared with CAJY (0.42 h-1). Table 1: CAJY with 2 vvm 1.76 +/- 0.01 g/L. Table 2: CAJY (**2 vvm) YP/S 0.039, YX/S 0.38, PP 0.176 g/L/h, Px 3.72 g/L/h, muXmax 0.42 h
growth_records.notes: S. zooepidemicus ATCC 39920 in CAJY, 3 L BioFLO III fermenter, 2.0 L, 2 vvm aeration, 37C, pH 7.0, 150 rpm. Reported (Table 2): muXmax 0.42 /h (highest of all conditions), YX/S 0.38, YP/S 0.039, PP 0.176 g/L/h, Px 3.72 g/L/h, muPmax 0.06 /h. td=0.693/0.42=1.65 h. Derived qS=muXmax/YX/S=0.42/0.38=1.105 g sugar/g cell/h (non-experimental). HA final 1.76 +/- 0.01 g/L; under aeration yeast-extract supplementation did NOT raise HA vs plain CAJ (1.88), though it maximized biomass/growth. Highest YX/S and Px of the study.
